Steady 50 FPS on High Settings. Game progress does not appear to sync.

notes: The game works very well with this setup, but browsing servers although works, as you select one or try to join to one, the game immediately crashes. Matchmaking for on-line games works. As does off-line solo mode

title: Killing Floor 2
notes:
1. For even load the game, you have to tweak game launch options (right click on the game in library -> properties -> set launch options) and write there "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%" without quotes.
2. Exps are not loaded nor saved.
3. Graphical glitches make the game almost unplayable. Tried low to high settings with no difference.

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX Vega (VEGA10, DRM 3.23.0, 4.15.0-33-generic, LLVM 8.0.0)
notes: Did not test gameplay, but it completely ruins your stats. Perks reset to level 0, and you have to reset stats in console with "resetstats steamname", and start it all over again.

notes:
Multiplayer doesn't seems to work.
Solo offline mode works perfectly.

notes: Crashes after a few minutes, corrupts your existing steamcloud save file.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: High framerate, but stutters constantly. There is no correlation between graphical settings and the stuttering, it does it on High and Low settings.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
Solo offline and online multiplayer works.
Connecting to LAN server crashes the game.
Performance is pretty good.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: using wineD3D11 causes intense graphical glitches, can use `-dx10` launch option to run the game normally with massively reduced framerate. Crashes on server browser, offline works fine.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Works, playable, but is kinda stuttery, and is far from smooth.

notes: Performs significantly worse than it does on Windows and stutters constantly, but is still playable. Selecting a server on the server browser crashes the game, but multiplayer is still possible through matchmaking. Singleplayer works fine.

notes: Bugsplat at Startup

notes: Works perfectly even in online mode (if not using the server browser)

title: Killing Floor 2

notes: I tried everything but the game gave me a seizure and this is how it looks like playing the game for me. https://youtu.be/ZIAjp2h6y4A
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
First of all: switching from Win to Linux broke my stats/perks, but i could fix it with deleting the steamcloudfile - after that all progress was restored!

I didn't do any tweaks (D3d11), because on my system they broke the game entirely. Runs "out of the box" but with poor performance (lagging/fps drops, especially when many things happen on the screen). Serverbrowser doesn't work, but you can que up to find games automatically.
It runs without other issues and you CAN play. Not so much fun with the fpsdrops tho...
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Game is playable without issue, but when clicking on game in game browser it crashes. Automatic search works.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: I have played the game extensively for well over a month (a total of 24 hours) on different proton releases. The game has always worked flawlessly for me with the exception of the server browser issue, and Nvidia FLEX not working. However Proton 3.16-4 Beta seems to have fixed the server browser issue, making the game work flawlessly out of the box.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: The issue with game browser is gone in current version of proton. Game is running without any issue and I spent hours of fun with it. :-)
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: All parts of the game work normally, including server browser.  Performance is significantly worse than Windows.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Game runs out-of-the-box with acceptable performance. Both player stats and server browser crash have been fixed. Slight stutter (could be shaders) but they're pretty bearable.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Perk progression has carried across perfectly (was being wiped under previous versions) and server browser no longer crashes the game. Performance is equivalent to Windows, none of the stuttering reported by other users. I played a couple of rounds online and offline, no issues. Steam overlay works  but I cannot see the mouse cursor for some reason.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Game works perfectly but performance is weak. Heavy stutters and locked 60 fps
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: I ran it for over 10 hours on a medium graphical setting. Avg. FPS of 80 to 90 with Slight stuttering. Some maps preform worse than others (monster ball and nuked) after the scripted events occur.  Firebug really killed my FPS down to 30 or so in nuked as well.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: If you are running a multiple monitor setup have it on full screen if you want to be able to tab out
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Only some minor graphical issues mainly in lens flare and light rays (I disabled that in graphical menu). Game runs on Ultra settings 1440p at +-35 FPS.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Played solo offline but with the new Proton version, the server browser works
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: The game, much like its cousin Red Orchestra 2, sometimes crashes after the load screen. When you get in and skip all the movies, there is a black screen for about 15 sec before the title screen loads. Even after getting in game, it is crash-prone, usually happens when making a map change.  If youre willing to tolerate these issues, its playable.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
Seemed to work perfectly, but my perks weren't levelling up after any games - neither the tutorial, nor online play would shift my perks from zero. This is apparently a random sync issue with Tripwire's server. I had to go to the game's console and type "resetstats username" to resync. I had no issues doing this, but obviously if you've played a while under windows and you get hit with this, it might be a problem.

As I said though, having done so, the game works perfectly and I haven't experienced even a single crash in nearly 10 hours of online gameplay.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
Increasing the file descriptor limit fixes some random crashes without error messages ( https://github.com/zfigura/wine/blob/esync/README.esync ).
Game runs almostly good as on Windows, with occasional frame drops, but overall pretty smooth performance with zen or liquorix kernel
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Had pretty much no problem running the game for an extended period, performance was good other than some occasional slowdown in menus.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
Runs perfectly without issues. Slight delay on startup though. The system may prompt for unresponsiveness at startup, please press "wait" it would take about 20 seconds for the game to start.

I tried the game with the default settings at first (I had no hope it would work). Now, I'm using "Ultra" settings for almost everything and it works even better ¯\_(ツ)_/
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: runs perfectly out of the box

notes: A little slow to load, but that's the only issue.

notes: Beside the buggy steam overlay, the game runs as good as native
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Playing on build 1075 my perks carried over fine. Game ran well on high gfx settings. Minor stutter here and there but unsure if that is related to the game, Proton, or combination thereof.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
The game will crash when you set graphics options higher than Low. It also crashes when returning to the main menu or when you join a server from a friend invitation.
Had to play with -dx10 to avoid crashing after startup screen.
However, one very good thing is that your perks progress and Vault are now syncing.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Game ran pretty much perfectly, I noticed a bit of performance difference from when I played this game on windows and it seems to have boosted my fps some on med/high settings. Disabled Esync to prevent reported random crashes.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: I played this game  on a Windows 7 machine for more than 496 hours... And about 2 hours on Linux via Proton 3.16-5. The performance is almost the same using the same GPU, a GTX 970: Solid 60 FPS with graphics settings on high.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes: The game crashes after startup. I tried "-windowed" flag since I'm on a dual screen setup but that didn't prevent if from crashing randomly. I tried disabling Esync without success.
